%%KC_BLOCK%%<div class="kc-visual-plan" data-visual-plan-b64="" style="display:none;"></div>%%KC_END%%
# B.3-2 The Exponentially Varying Sinusoid

> **Objective:** Recognize, read, and sketch signals of the form \(x(t) = Ke^{-at}\cos(\omega_0 t + \theta)\) quickly and accurately under exam conditions.

The fastest exam pattern: take \(x(t) = 4e^{-2t}\cos(6t - 60^\circ)\) and split it into two parts — the exponential \(4e^{-2t}\) and the cosine \(\cos(6t - 60^\circ)\). Draw the upper and lower envelopes \(\pm 4e^{-2t}\), then fit the shifted cosine oscillation between them. That is the entire sketching strategy. Read \(K\), \(a\), \(\omega_0\), and \(\theta\) directly from the formula — no derivation needed. These questions reward fast parameter reading, not long algebra.

$$x(t) = Ke^{-at} \cos(\omega_0 t + \theta)$$
*This signal is a cosine whose amplitude is not constant but is instead controlled by the decaying exponential envelope \(Ke^{-at}\), which shrinks the oscillation toward zero as \(t\) increases.*

%%KC_BLOCK%%<div class="kc-visual-meta" data-visual-kind="generate_image" data-teaching-role="concept_anchor" data-visual-use-b64="" style="display:none;"></div>%%KC_END%%
*🎨 Three-step sketch strategy: draw the decaying envelope, identify the shifted cosine parameters, then fit the oscillation between the upper and lower bounds.*
![Illustration](/generated/gptimage2-1777214725385-2131.png)

## 1. Fast Sketch Rule

Apply this four-step checklist to \(x(t) = 4e^{-2t}\cos(6t - 60^\circ)\) — or any signal in this form — without deriving anything.

**Step 1 — Read the exponential part:** The exponent is \(-2t\), so \(a = 2\) and the time constant is \(\tau = 1/a = 0.5\) s. The signal decays to roughly 37% of its initial value after \(0.5\) s.

**Step 2 — Read the cosine part:** Angular frequency \(\omega_0 = 6\) rad/s, so period \(T_0 = 2\pi/6 \approx 1\) s. The phase is \(-60^\circ\), which is a delay of \(60/360 \approx 1/6\) of one cycle, or about \(1/6\) s.

**Step 3 — Draw the envelopes:** Sketch \(+4e^{-2t}\) and \(-4e^{-2t}\) as dashed bounding curves.

**Step 4 — Fit the sinusoid:** Draw the cosine oscillation between those envelopes, shifted right by \(\approx 1/6\) s.

> Do not derive anything unless the problem explicitly asks for it.

$$\tau = \frac{1}{a}, \qquad T_0 = \frac{2\pi}{\omega_0}$$
*These are the two essential speed-reading formulas: \(\tau\) tells you how fast the amplitude decays, and \(T_0\) tells you how fast the signal oscillates.*

## 2. What the Envelopes Mean — and the Main Traps

The curves \(+Ke^{-at}\) and \(-Ke^{-at}\) are **amplitude bounds**, not separate signals. The waveform touches \(+Ke^{-at}\) exactly at the positive peaks of the cosine (where \(\cos = 1\)) and touches \(-Ke^{-at}\) exactly at the negative peaks (where \(\cos = -1\)).

### COMMON TRAPS

**Trap 1 — Amplitude decay \(\neq\) frequency change.** As \(t\) increases, the oscillations get smaller, but they do not get slower. The period \(T_0 = 2\pi/\omega_0\) is fixed by \(\omega_0\) alone and does not change.

**Trap 2 — Phase shift must become a time shift when sketching.** A phase of \(-60^\circ\) means the waveform is delayed by \((60/360)\,T_0\) seconds. Convert degrees to a fraction of the period before placing the first peak on your sketch.

**Trap 3 — \(Ke^{-at}\) is not the signal.** The exponential envelope only controls the height. The cosine factor still determines every zero crossing, every peak location, and the direction of each oscillation.

---
**📌 Key Takeaways**
- Sketch by drawing envelopes \(\pm Ke^{-at}\) first, then fit the shifted cosine between them.
- Use \(\tau = 1/a\) for decay rate and \(T_0 = 2\pi/\omega_0\) for oscillation period.
- Decay shrinks amplitude only — the period never changes with the exponential factor.

*In the next section we will extend this idea to complex exponentials and see how the exponentially varying sinusoid connects to the general form \(e^{st}\) used throughout system analysis.*

%%KC_BLOCK%%<div class="kc-quiz-plan" data-quiz-b64="" style="display:none;"></div>%%KC_END%%
